subagent_reports.topic: CLI command bridge architecture, conversion candidate tiers, and OpenCode shell-output bridge semantics for addCliCommandBridges

sources.label: OpenCode Commands docs (official)
sources.summary: Confirms !`command` injects bash command OUTPUT into the prompt; commands run in project root; output becomes part of the prompt sent to the LLM. Framework injects output — it does NOT enforce verbatim/no-analysis. That discipline is an agent-prompt convention, which is why adv-status.md carries explicit 'return verbatim / do not analyze' instructions plus an asset test.

sources.label: OpenCode commands.mdx source (anomalyco/opencode dev)
sources.summary: Authoritative source for template processing: shell execution via !`command`, $ARGUMENTS placeholder, custom command can override built-in. Confirms bridge mechanism is stable and documented.

sources.label: Bun parseArgs guide (official, oven-sh/bun)
sources.summary: Idiomatic zero-dependency CLI parsing is node:util parseArgs with options + strict + allowPositionals. bin/adv already follows this exact pattern. Boring-tech baseline; commander is the heavier escalation only when multi-subcommand dispatch outgrows parseArgs.

sources.label: Local: bin/adv (current CLI)
sources.summary: Standalone Bun script, single `status` subcommand. Resolves projectId from git root-commit SHA, reads external JSON projections under $XDG_DATA_HOME/opencode/plugins/advance/{projectId}, pure compute helpers (computeLastActivity, classifyRecency, gate progress), no-color stable rendering, explicit exit codes (0 ok / 1 git-or-unknown / 2 no-state). Duplicates gate order + lastActivity algorithm from plugin/src (cited inline as source-of-truth references).

sources.label: Local: advance-meta spec rq-statusCliBridge01
sources.summary: MUST-priority law. .1 CLI table injection no-color; .2 forbids agent fanout (adv_status/list/show/gate_status/spec) and synthesized health/freshness/recommendations; .3 CLI failure surfaces stderr and MUST NOT fall back to ADV MCP tools. Governing precedent for every new bridge.

sources.label: Local: adv-status-cli-assets.test.ts
sources.summary: Pins command body: contains !`adv status --no-color`, requires verbatim/do-not-analyze/no-recommendations, forbidden-token list (adv_status, adv_change_list, ...), spec anchoring. Reusable regression-guard template for any new bridge command.

sources.label: Local: plugin/src/tools/roadmap.ts + backlog.ts
sources.summary: roadmap.ts exposes pure helpers: readSnapshotFile, assessAnnotationFreshness, filter/sort/group, live gh project read. backlog.ts adv_backlog_state composes them with TTL freshness + O(1) Visibility annotation. Active-change annotation REQUIRES Temporal Visibility (queryActiveChangesByIssueNumbers) — a disk-only CLI bridge cannot reproduce it without a store-list fallback (buildActiveChangeIndexFromStore is the O(n*m) fallback).

sources.label: Local: command bodies inspected
sources.summary: adv-validate is thin (single adv_change_validate call + display tables). adv-roadmap is prompt-heavy render + active-change cross-ref + recommendations. adv-cleanup/slop-scan/arch-scan are dry-run/Phase-1-deterministic but interleave Tier B HITL approval, sub-agent fanout, judgment — only their deterministic pre-flight/enumeration slices are bridge-safe.

subagent_reports.architecture_assessment: EXISTING PATTERN: One proven CLI bridge (/adv-status -> `adv status --no-color`) backed by a standalone disk-reading Bun script, a MUST spec (rq-statusCliBridge01), and an asset test. Bridge reads on-disk JSON projections only; no Temporal, no MCP, no network. REFERENCE PATTERN (by-the-book): OpenCode docs confirm !`command` shell-output injection is the canonical deterministic-output mechanism; node:util parseArgs is the canonical zero-dependency Bun CLI parser. ADV is squarely on the boring/proven path. DEVIATION: NONE for existing bridge. EXPANSION RISKS: (1) State-reader duplication — bin/adv hand-reimplements gate order + lastActivity from plugin/src; each new disk-reading subcommand widens drift + grows a monolithic file. (2) Projection staleness — on-disk change.json is a DOWNSTREAM projection updated only on terminal/gate transitions (AGENTS.md 'Signal-driven change workflows'); per-change Temporal workflow state is source of truth. Pure-disk CLI cannot serve data needing live Temporal/Visibility (active-change annotation, worktree/session/WIP, in-flight gate granularity). (3) HITL erosion — converting any slice owning Tier B approval, gate ownership, cancellation, archive, acceptance into an autonomous CLI breaches rq-statusCliBridge01-class no-fanout AND human-authority invariants. (4) Failure-fallback regression — rq-statusCliBridge01.3 forbids MCP fallback on CLI error; new bridges must replicate 'surface stderr, do not re-enter agent fanout' or they silently re-introduce fanout.

validation.notes: Bridge mechanism, CLI parser choice, and no-fallback failure contract are VALIDATED against official OpenCode docs, Bun docs, and the existing MUST spec. CONCERNS (non-blocking) are scoping/guardrails, not feasibility: keep Temporal-dependent reads agent-owned or behind explicit opt-in tools; do not let bin/adv re-derive plugin algorithms without a shared source-of-truth. Conversion of HITL/gate/archive/acceptance/judgment work is an ANTI-PATTERN and stays agent-owned.

subagent_reports.recommendation: RECOMMENDED FIRST STEP (lowest risk): add `adv roadmap` as a disk-only read of `.adv/roadmap-snapshot.json` reusing existing network/Temporal-free helpers (readSnapshotFile + assessAnnotationFreshness + pure filter/sort) WITHOUT active-change annotation; bridge /adv-roadmap's non-annotated read path to it. Keep active-change cross-reference + recommendations agent-owned (they need Temporal Visibility). ALTERNATIVE: /adv-validate is thin but adv_change_validate likely needs live workflow/spec state — if so, make it a thin single-MCP-call command (one adv_change_validate + deterministic table, no fanout, no recommendations) mirroring status discipline, NOT a pure-disk CLI. MANDATORY GUARDRAILS (mirror rq-statusCliBridge01): (a) New spec rq-<cmd>CliBridge01 with .1 output injection, .2 forbids agent fanout/synthesis, .3 CLI failure surfaces stderr with NO MCP fallback. (b) New *-cli-assets.test.ts cloned from adv-status-cli-assets.test.ts asserting the !`adv <subcmd>` token, verbatim/no-analysis instructions, forbidden-token list, spec anchoring. (c) Refactor bin/adv BEFORE adding subcommand #2: extract shared disk-read + projection-shape helpers (or codegen shape from plugin/src/types.ts) so gate order / lastActivity / snapshot schema have ONE source of truth. (d) Mark each disk-read subcommand's known-staleness boundary in --help + command body. (e) Do NOT convert any slice owning Tier B approval, gates, cancellation, archive, acceptance, sub-agent dispatch, or judgment.
follow_ups: Confirm whether adv_change_validate can execute purely from on-disk change.json + .adv/specs without Temporal/Visibility; decides CLI-bridge vs thin single-MCP-call for /adv-validate.
Decide source-of-truth strategy for bin/adv projection shapes (shared helper module vs codegen from plugin/src/types.ts) BEFORE adding subcommand #2 to prevent gate-order/lastActivity drift.
Catalog Temporal-dependent command slices (active-change annotation, WIP/worktree/session, live gate granularity) so they are explicitly excluded from disk-only CLI bridges.
Author reusable spec + asset-test templates so each future bridge inherits the no-fanout + no-fallback contract by construction (structural-correctness over prose).

subagent_reports.architecture_assessment: bin/adv establishes the canonical CLI-bridge shape: pure disk-projection reads of change.json under the XDG external root, git-root-SHA project resolution, zero Temporal, zero mutation, stable no-color output, numbered exit codes. rq-statusCliBridge01 (verified by adv-status-cli-assets.test.ts) is the reference contract: the slash command becomes a thin verbatim bridge with MCP fanout forbidden, while richer/diagnostic reads stay on the MCP tool. CLI-safe boundary: deterministic reads of disk projections (changes, roadmap snapshot, in-repo specs, reflections, agenda, wisdom, project metadata), dry-run/read-only scans (snapshot scan, conformance status), read-only diagnostics (temporal diagnose). Dangerous boundary: anything that fires a Temporal signal, completes a gate, mutates tasks, archives/git-finalizes, or requires HITL — must stay agent-owned because state lives in Temporal workflows (signal/query-only surface) and disk change.json is only a downstream projection updated on terminal/gate transitions. Critical nuance: adv_change_validate/show read Temporal-first (store.changes.get), so a CLI port needs either a shared disk-projection read helper extracted from bin/adv's listChanges/loadChangeJson, or explicit disk-only semantics with a freshness disclaimer, to avoid duplicating state logic and silent divergence from live workflow state.

validation.notes: No anti-pattern. Boring/proven path matches the already-shipped bin/adv + rq-statusCliBridge01 reference exactly. Sequencing roadmap next is correct: pure file-snapshot read with existing freshness contract and no Temporal dependency, lowest-risk after status.

subagent_reports.recommendation: Ship CLI bridges only for pure disk-projection reads and read-only diagnostics, in order: (1) adv roadmap (reads .adv/roadmap-snapshot.json; --json for CI), (2) adv validate --json as CI gate (disk-projection-only, freshness-disclaimed), (3) adv doctor (temporal diagnose + snapshot scan, read-only), (4) adv spec list/show (read in-repo .adv/specs directly). Extract a shared read helper module (project resolution + change.json/spec loaders) from bin/adv so status/roadmap/validate/doctor do not each reimplement state logic. Keep all gate-owning workflow commands and all signal-firing/HITL/destructive tools (gate_complete, task add/update/cancel/checkpoint, change create/close/archive/reenter, contract_mint, worktree create/delete, temporal worker_restart/reconnect/register, snapshot repair, conformance init/lock/unlock/override) strictly agent-owned with NO CLI mutation authority. Conformance status/run and snapshot scan may expose CLI read/CI-JSON only.
follow_ups: adv_change_validate CLI port requires a decision: extract disk-projection read helper vs. disk-only-with-freshness-disclaimer, since the MCP tool reads Temporal-first.
Add a CI JSON mode (--json) to validate/roadmap/doctor so CI consumes structured verdicts without ANSI parsing; gate with an assets test mirroring rq-statusCliBridge01.
Conformance run already reads a CI artifact — a thin adv conformance status --json could surface last_verdict in CI without granting lock/override authority.

sources.summary: Attempt 1 recommends roadmap bridge first + extract shared bin/adv helpers BEFORE subcommand #2. Attempt 2 sequences roadmap > validate --json > doctor > spec and flags adv_change_validate/show read Temporal-FIRST (store.changes.get), so a disk-only port diverges from live state. Both flag bin/adv state-reader duplication as the top expansion risk.

subagent_reports.architecture_assessment: Approved scope is on the boring/proven path: one shipped bridge (/adv-status -> `adv status --no-color`) backed by a MUST spec + asset test. The discovery surface has ALREADY been researched twice; this scout's job is to surface what those passes under-weighted as actionable opportunities, not re-litigate feasibility. Central architectural tension confirmed by direct evidence: bin/adv is fully standalone (zero plugin/src imports) and hand-duplicates GATE_ORDER + lastActivity, while the exact reusable helpers for the recommended-first roadmap bridge (readSnapshotFile, assessAnnotationFreshness) live in plugin/src and are Temporal-free but not importable across the build boundary. That boundary decision (codegen shapes vs. shared bundled module vs. re-duplicate) is the highest-leverage unmade decision and gates whether subcommand #2 compounds drift or amortizes it. Two opportunities the prior passes mentioned only as follow-ups deserve elevation to contract items: (a) a parameterized/shared bridge-contract asset-test harness so each new bridge inherits no-fanout+no-fallback BY CONSTRUCTION (structural-correctness P33) instead of hand-cloning prose; (b) the --json/CI-JSON mode as a FIRST-CLASS acceptance surface (proposal Success Criteria already names ci-json as a classification but no AC pins a structured-verdict contract). One missed edge case: rq-statusCliBridge01.3's no-MCP-fallback invariant must be replicated per bridge or new bridges silently re-introduce fanout on CLI error — this is a failure-mode AC gap, not just a guardrail.

validation.notes: No anti-pattern. Candidates are contract-tied opportunities/leverage points within approved scope; none expand objectives or touch constraints/avoidances. All HITL/gate/destructive/signal work stays agent-owned per scope guardrail.

subagent_reports.recommendation: Adopt the bin/adv shared-helper extraction (C1) and the parameterized bridge-contract harness (C2) now — both are contract-tied, low-risk, and prevent compounding drift before the first new (roadmap) bridge lands. Design around C3 (ci-json verdict contract) by adding a --json acceptance criterion. Defer C4 (doctor/diagnostics bridge) to follow-up; surface C5 (validate disk-only-vs-Temporal-first divergence) to the orchestrator/user because it carries a freshness/correctness tradeoff that is a user-value decision, not auto-adoptable.
follow_ups: TASK_SCOPE anchors present in packet; no packet defects. Proposal still carries CLARIFY_MISSING_SUCCESS_CRITERIA — C3 (ci-json verdict contract) directly answers the missing measurable-outcome gap.

Orchestrator must decide bin/adv cross-boundary strategy (codegen from plugin/src/types.ts vs. bundled shared module vs. continued duplication) BEFORE roadmap subcommand lands — this is the keystone leverage point.

subagent_reports.architecture_assessment: The design is a faithful, lower-risk clone of the already-shipped /adv-status CLI bridge pattern (rq-statusCliBridge01). All four load-bearing claims are independently verified against source: (1) plugin roadmap.ts is dependency-poisoned by zod/temporal/store, so runtime import into a zero-dep CLI is impossible — parity tests are the correct mechanism; (2) GATE_ORDER and RoadmapSnapshot/assessRoadmapFreshness are real exported parity targets; (3) the existing asset test is structured for parameterization per AC6; (4) bin/adv already uses node:util parseArgs with subcommand dispatch. The design correctly omits/labels active-change annotation (Temporal Visibility dependency) rather than faking it from disk (DONT4). The one genuine user-value cost — default /adv-roadmap losing live active-change cross-reference — is explicitly surfaced in Decision 5, not hidden.

validation.notes: No spec conflicts, no security/release blockers, no contract compromise. Three non-blocking cautions recorded (annotation-loss UX tradeoff already surfaced; pre-existing bin/adv doc-comment drift; parity-test scope must cover both freshness functions). Verdict: VALIDATED.

subagent_reports.recommendation: Proceed. The design satisfies AC1-AC9 and respects all four DONT constraints with verified evidence. Recommend two prep-task refinements: (a) ensure the GATE_ORDER parity test imports from plugin/src/types/gates.ts (authoritative) and, as a campsite fix, correct the stale bin/adv:23 comment that says 'plugin/src/types.ts GATE_DEFS' to name gates.ts; (b) scope the roadmap snapshot parity test to cover BOTH RoadmapSnapshot shape AND assessRoadmapFreshness 2h threshold so freshness-text drift fails CI, since AC4 ties file-mode staleness wording to that constant. The default-path annotation-loss tradeoff is real but was correctly surfaced at the design checkpoint; no change required beyond the one-line MCP opt-in pointer already in the design.
follow_ups: Confirm the parameterized asset harness asserts a forbidden-token list for roadmap that includes adv_roadmap, adv_backlog_state, adv_change_list (mirroring the status forbidden list) so the bridge cannot regress into fanout.

The deferred adv validate disk-vs-Temporal research (AC8) is correctly out of scope here; ensure the design's recorded validateChange purity finding (validator.ts:104 pure function) is carried into the follow-up change.

subagent_reports.topic: Validate CLI status bridge design: live Temporal-backed `adv status`, thin `/adv-status`, no silent disk fallback, no mutation

sources.label: Temporal TS SDK WorkflowHandle.query (official)
sources.summary: query<Ret,Args>(def: QueryDefinition | string, ...args) — query-by-NAME-STRING is officially supported: `await handle.query<number,[]>('getValue')`. CLI can call handle.query('adv.change.getState') WITHOUT importing the workflow definition module.

sources.label: plugin/src/temporal/messages.ts
sources.summary: changeStateQuery=getStateQuery defined via wf.defineQuery; module imports `@temporalio/workflow` (sandbox runtime). A plain client/CLI process CANNOT import this module — confirms query-name-string is the correct boundary.

sources.label: plugin/src/temporal/contracts.ts
sources.summary: CHANGE_WORKFLOW_QUERY_NAMES.getState='adv.change.getState' (stable string constant). ChangeWorkflowState carries tasks, gates, status, title, createdAt, fast_follow_of — every field bin/adv renders is available from the live query payload.

sources.label: plugin/src/temporal/client.ts + list-change-workflows.ts
sources.summary: createTemporalClientBundle (loopback guard, namespace validation) + listChangeWorkflowIds (Visibility query AdvAffectedProjects + AdvChangeStatus IN default non-terminal). Canonical live-enumeration path the CLI should mirror, not re-derive.

sources.label: plugin/src/storage/store-temporal/changes.ts listSummary + shared.ts QUERY_TIMEOUT_MS
sources.summary: Existing warm path unions memo+Visibility+disk and falls back to disk on Visibility failure (the exact anti-pattern AC4 forbids for the CLI). 5s per-query timeout exists because a dead worker hangs queries indefinitely — CLI MUST bound its own queries.

subagent_reports.architecture_assessment: Design is sound and aligns with boring/proven patterns. The hard architectural law to enforce: the CLI is a Temporal CLIENT process, not a workflow-sandbox context, so it must reach live state via (1) createTemporalClientBundle conventions, (2) listChangeWorkflowIds-style Visibility query, (3) handle.query('adv.change.getState') by NAME STRING — never importing messages.ts/workflows. Temporal docs confirm string-name query is first-class API, so no plugin-source import is needed and the bin/plugin boundary stays clean. Render inputs (tasks/gates/status/title) are all present on ChangeWorkflowState. The plugin's own listSummary deliberately disk-falls-back on Visibility failure; the CLI must do the OPPOSITE per AC4 (fail non-zero, no silent disk active rows). Bounded per-query timeout is mandatory — mirror QUERY_TIMEOUT_MS (5s) or the CLI hangs on a dead worker exactly as the plugin warned.

validation.notes: No anti-patterns. One must-fix to avoid regression: bound every Temporal connect/list/query with an explicit timeout (5s precedent) and treat timeout as the AC4 non-zero+remediation path, not a disk fallback.

subagent_reports.recommendation: Adopt the draft. Enforce 3 laws in spec rq-statusCliBridge01: (1) CLI queries live state by query-name STRING 'adv.change.getState' via CHANGE_WORKFLOW_QUERY_NAMES.getState — never import messages.ts/workflows (sandbox-only, breaks the client). (2) Active rows come ONLY from live workflow queries; disk JSON permitted ONLY for archived/closed counts. (3) Connect/list/query failure or timeout => exit non-zero + remediation + JSON {source:'temporal',live:false,stale:false,error,remediation}; never substitute disk active rows. Mirror createTemporalClientBundle (loopback guard) + listChangeWorkflowIds (Visibility AdvAffectedProjects + AdvChangeStatus) + a 5s bounded timeout. Tests: Bun unit tests with mocked live client (success + connect-fail + query-timeout) and a plugin asset test asserting adv-status.md stays verbatim no-fanout. Do NOT import bin helpers into plugin tests except for text/parity.
follow_ups: Duplicate the query-name constant ('adv.change.getState') as a bin-local literal with a comment pinning it to CHANGE_WORKFLOW_QUERY_NAMES.getState; add a plugin parity test asserting the two strings match so drift is caught without a runtime import.

Confirm the Bun CLI can resolve @temporalio/client at runtime (bin/adv currently has zero deps); adding the client dep is the one new surface — verify it does not pull the workflow sandbox transitively.

Pattern: (1) unit-test bin logic with Bun's runner — `bin/lib/*.test.ts` run via `bun test bin/`, wired as a dedicated CI step (Bun already installed in CI via oven-sh/setup-bun); (2) cross-boundary contract/parity/coverage tests live in plugin/src as vitest and read bin files as TEXT (readFileSync + regex), importing only in-tree plugin constants — exactly how plugin/src/adv-status-cli-assets.test.ts works. Never import bin/lib into plugin tests.

documents.proposal:
# Add CLI Command Bridges

## Why

`/adv-status` was converted to a thin CLI bridge over `adv status --no-color`, reducing agent context use and avoiding unnecessary MCP fanout. Other ADV commands/tools still ask the agent to perform deterministic reads, validation, bucketing, diagnostics, and table rendering that a CLI/CI surface could own.

The core deliverable is a **factor-driven decision matrix**: for every command and tool, evaluate (a) agent in-context need, (b) programmability / CI value, (c) human-direct value, (d) mutation / safety — and derive the ideal surface. The right form may be: keep as an MCP tool, add an additive CLI surface alongside MCP, **move the primary path to CLI and retire the MCP form when the matrix justifies it**, or remain an agent-only workflow. Gate ownership, HITL approvals, destructive actions, Temporal-signal mutations, and judgment-heavy workflows stay agent-owned.

Replacement is permitted, not forbidden — but only when the matrix shows the agent does not need the tool's structured output in-context AND a CLI fully supersedes its value AND no command/workflow depends on it.

## What Changes

- Build a factor-driven decision matrix over every `/adv-*` command and registered ADV MCP tool.
- Score each on: agent in-context need (ACN), programmability/CI value (PROG), human value (HUM), mutation/safety.
- Derive a disposition per tool/command from the factors.
- Implement selected deterministic CLI subcommands or CI JSON outputs.
- Convert approved slash commands into short shell-output bridges where safe.
- Retire an MCP tool only when its matrix row justifies it; otherwise retain.
- Add specs/tests pinning bridge no-fanout contracts and pinning any MCP-tool retirement to a documented matrix justification.
- Preserve agent workflows for gates, approvals, task/code mutation, archive, and review/harden judgment.

## Decision Factor Model

The audit's primary deliverable. Each row scored, then a disposition derived.

### Factors (per tool / command)

| Factor | Values | Meaning |
|---|---|---|
| ACN (agent in-context need) | HIGH / MED / LOW | Does the agent need this tool's structured output in-context to make a workflow decision? |
| PROG (programmability/CI) | HIGH / MED / LOW | Value as a scriptable CLI/CI output (exit codes, `--json`). |
| HUM (human-direct value) | HIGH / MED / LOW | Value to a human running it directly in a terminal. |
| RISK (mutation/safety) | READ / MUTATE | Read-only vs mutation/HITL/destructive/signal. |

### Disposition derivation

| Disposition | Rule |
|---|---|
| `no-cli-dangerous` | RISK = MUTATE. MCP-only forever; never a CLI surface. |
| `keep-mcp-only` | READ, ACN = HIGH, PROG/HUM ≤ MED. Agent needs it in-context; little standalone CLI value. |
| `mcp+cli-additive` | READ, ACN ≥ MED AND PROG/HUM ≥ MED. Keep MCP for agents; ADD CLI/CI surface. |
| `cli-primary` | READ, ACN = LOW, PROG/HUM = HIGH. Move the default/agent-facing path to CLI; underlying MCP tool retained only for opt-in depth. |
| `cli-replace` | READ, ACN = LOW, PROG/HUM = HIGH, AND no command/workflow needs the MCP tool. CLI supersedes; MCP tool may be retired with documented justification. |

### Command dispositions

| Disposition | Meaning |
|---|---|
| `cli-bridge-primary` | Slash command becomes a thin `!\`adv …\`` bridge so the agent stops rendering deterministically in-context. |
| `mcp+cli-additive` | Command stays agent-driven; a CLI/CI subcommand (e.g. `--json`) is added for its deterministic slice. |
| `agent-workflow-only` | Gate/HITL/judgment command; no CLI form. |

## Success Criteria

1. Research covers every current `/adv-*` command and every registered ADV MCP tool.
2. Each row carries factor scores (ACN, PROG, HUM, RISK) and a derived disposition with rationale.
3. The matrix explicitly states, per tool, whether its MCP form is kept, kept-with-additive-CLI, moved to CLI, or retired — and why.
4. Any proposed MCP-tool retirement (`cli-replace`) cites matrix evidence (ACN=LOW, full CLI supersession, zero workflow dependency).
5. At least one approved deterministic surface is implemented as a CLI bridge or CLI/CI subcommand.
6. Converted bridge templates return CLI output verbatim with no normal-path MCP fanout/analysis/fallback.
7. Tests/specs assert bridge no-fanout contracts and assert that any MCP-tool retirement is matrix-justified (no accidental removal of an agent-needed tool).
8. Tier A/Tier B approvals, gate completion, destructive cleanup, archive/git, and code/task mutation remain agent-owned with no CLI mutation authority.

## Acceptance Criteria

- AC1: Discovery/design artifacts contain the full factor matrix (ACN/PROG/HUM/RISK + disposition) for all commands and tools.
- AC2: Every row has a disposition value and rationale.
- AC3: Each `cli-replace` row documents the supersession + zero-workflow-dependency justification; non-replace read tools document why MCP is retained.
- AC4: Every recommended conversion cites at least one local source path.
- AC5: Implementation changes at least one selected deterministic command or CLI subcommand.
- AC6: Each converted bridge contains a shell-output bridge with a no-color/stable flag and no normal-path MCP fanout instructions.
- AC7: Asset tests fail if a converted bridge reintroduces agent analysis/recommendations/MCP fanout, OR if an MCP tool is removed without a recorded matrix justification.
- AC8: Existing gate/HITL command files retain Tier A/Tier B approval language.

## Scope

### In Scope

- Factor matrix + disposition decisions for all commands/tools.
- CLI bridges for deterministic/read-only command surfaces.
- Additive CLI/CI JSON outputs for deterministic phases.
- Read-only diagnostics: status, roadmap, spec, doctor/health, conformance status, snapshot scan, worktree/session inventory.
- Dry-run-only CLI paths where mutation exists but preview is safe.
- Matrix-justified MCP-tool retirement where applicable.
- Spec/test patterns keeping bridges thin and pinning retirements.

### Out of Scope

- Replacing collaborative gate workflows wholesale.
- Removing MCP tools that agents/workflows still need (un-justified removal).
- Bypassing Tier A/Tier B approvals.
- Auto-executing destructive cleanup, archive, issue creation, git push, gate completion, or task mutation from CLI output alone.
- Broad CLI framework rewrite unrelated to selected conversions.

### Must Not

- Do not convert workflows where agent judgment owns correctness, drift, acceptance, or user-value tradeoffs.
- Do not make heuristic/LLM output authoritative for safety-critical state or spec compliance.
- Do not use disk projections to authorize gate/task/archive/destructive mutations.
- Do not break existing `/adv-status` bridge contract.
- Do not retire an MCP tool without a recorded matrix justification.

## Constraints

- Preserve P33 structural correctness: typed args, parsers, schemas, tests, specs own correctness.
- Use `--no-color` or equivalent stable rendering for bridges; add `--json` for CI surfaces.
- CLI must document when it reads lagging disk projections instead of Temporal state.
- Prefer shared helper extraction over duplicating projection readers/rendering.

## Affected Code

- `bin/adv`
- `.opencode/command/adv-*.md`
- `plugin/src/manifest.ts`
- `plugin/src/tool-registry.ts` (only if a matrix-justified retirement lands)
- `plugin/src/*-assets.test.ts`
- `.adv/specs/advance-meta/spec.json`
- `plugin/src/tools/*` only if pure helpers are extracted/shared

## Research Validation

### Summary

Research validates the CLI-bridge architecture and reframes the deliverable as a factor matrix that yields per-tool dispositions, with matrix-justified replacement allowed. Safe path: ship CLI for deterministic read/diagnostic/dry-run work; keep Temporal-signal, HITL, gate, archive, and judgment work MCP/agent-owned.

### External Evidence

- OpenCode Commands docs (`https://opencode.ai/docs/commands/`): markdown → prompt template; `!\`command\`` injects bash output; verbatim/no-analysis enforced by ADV text+spec+test, not the framework.
- Bun argv (`https://bun.sh/guides/process/argv`) + Context7 `/oven-sh/bun`: `node:util parseArgs` is the supported zero-dep Bun CLI parser; `bin/adv` already uses it.

### Local Evidence

- `bin/adv`: standalone Bun CLI; git-root-SHA id; reads external JSON projections; `--no-color`; exit codes; 0 plugin imports (duplicates GATE_ORDER + lastActivity).
- `.opencode/command/adv-status.md` + `plugin/src/adv-status-cli-assets.test.ts:28-87`; `.adv/specs/advance-meta/spec.json:94-147` (`rq-statusCliBridge01`).
- `plugin/src/tool-registry.ts:704-759` (canonical tool list).
- `plugin/src/tools/roadmap.ts:647-729`, `backlog.ts:360-444`, `spec.ts:16-91`, `temporal-ops.ts:328-400`, `snapshot.ts:37-124`, `conformance.ts:8-24`, `change.ts` (Temporal-first reads).

### Discovery Opportunity Scout

- C1 (adopt-now): extract shared disk-read core from `bin/adv` before roadmap subcommand #2.
- C2 (adopt-now): parameterized bridge-contract asset-test harness (no-fanout + no-fallback + retirement-justification by construction).
- C3 (design-around): first-class `--json`/CI verdict AC (closes open `CLARIFY_MISSING_SUCCESS_CRITERIA`).
- C4 (defer): `adv doctor` read-only bridge after roadmap.
- C5 (surface-to-user): `adv validate` disk-only vs Temporal-first divergence — user decision.

## Command Matrix

| Command | ACN | PROG | HUM | RISK | Classification | Disposition |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| `/adv-status` | MED | HIGH | HIGH | READ | `cli-bridge` | `cli-bridge-primary` (shipped) |
| `/adv-roadmap` | MED | HIGH | HIGH | READ | `cli-bridge`+`ci-json` | `cli-bridge-primary` (next) |
| `/adv-validate` | HIGH | HIGH | MED | READ | `ci-json` | `mcp+cli-additive` (pending C5) |
| `/adv-triage` | MED | MED | MED | MUTATE (HITL) | `cli-helper` | `agent-workflow-only` (CLI helper slices) |
| `/adv-cleanup` | MED | MED | MED | MUTATE (HITL) | `cli-helper` | `agent-workflow-only` (dry-run helper) |
| `/adv-audit` | MED | HIGH | MED | READ | `ci-json` | `mcp+cli-additive` (deterministic phases) |
| `/adv-slop-scan` | LOW | HIGH | MED | READ | `ci-json` | `mcp+cli-additive` (Phase 1) |
| `/adv-arch-scan` | LOW | HIGH | MED | READ | `ci-json` | `mcp+cli-additive` (stack-pack phase) |
| `/adv-reflect` | MED | LOW | LOW | MUTATE | `cli-helper` | `agent-workflow-only` |
| `/adv-tron` | MED | LOW | MED | READ | `cli-helper` | `agent-workflow-only` |
| `/adv-improve`,`/adv-comp-scan` | HIGH | LOW | MED | READ | `agent-owned` | `agent-workflow-only` |
| gate/HITL commands (`proposal`,`idea`,`problem`,`clarify`,`research`,`discover`,`design`,`prep`,`apply`,`review`,`harden`,`archive`,`task`,`atc`,`refactor`) | HIGH | LOW | LOW | MUTATE | `agent-owned` | `agent-workflow-only` |

## Tool Matrix

| Tool/group | ACN | PROG | HUM | RISK | Disposition | Why this form |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| `adv_status` | MED | HIGH | HIGH | READ | `cli-primary` | CLI table shipped; MCP kept for `view:"health"` depth |
| `adv_roadmap`, `adv_backlog_state` | MED | HIGH | HIGH | READ | `cli-primary` | CLI file mode; MCP kept for live + Temporal annotation |
| `adv_spec` | HIGH | MED | MED | READ | `mcp+cli-additive` | Agents query specs mid-workflow; add CLI read |
| `adv_change_list`, `adv_change_show` | HIGH | MED | MED | READ | `mcp+cli-additive` | Agents need Temporal-first reads; CLI is human/CI snapshot w/ freshness caveat |
| `adv_change_validate` | HIGH | HIGH | MED | READ | `mcp+cli-additive` | Gates/archive need MCP; add CLI/CI verdict (C5 path) |
| `adv_task_list/show/ready` | HIGH | LOW | LOW | READ | `keep-mcp-only` | Agent-workflow reads; low standalone CLI value |

| `adv_wisdom_list`, `adv_project_wisdom_list` | HIGH | LOW | LOW | READ | `keep-mcp-only` | Agent knowledge surface |
| `adv_agenda_list` | MED | LOW | LOW | READ | `keep-mcp-only` | Agent-facing |

| `adv_reflect`, `adv_run_test`, `adv_task_checkpoint`, `adv_subagent_report_submit` | HIGH | LOW | LOW | MUTATE | `keep-mcp-only` | Workflow/evidence-bound agent tools |
| `adv_change_create/update/close/bulk_close/archive/update_issues/reenter` | HIGH | LOW | LOW | MUTATE | `no-cli-dangerous` | Mutation/approval/archive/git/spec |

Note: `cli-replace` (retire MCP form) is not triggered by any current read tool — all read tools retain ACN ≥ MED or have workflow dependencies. Replacement, if any, is applied at the COMMAND default-path level (`cli-bridge-primary`), not by deleting agent tools, unless a future row scores ACN=LOW with zero workflow dependency.

## CLI Namespace Recommendation

```text
adv status                 # shipped
adv roadmap [--json]       # snapshot backlog; Temporal annotation omitted/degraded
adv validate [--json]      # CI verdict (pending C5 decision)
adv spec list|show|search  # in-repo specs
adv doctor [--json]        # temporal diagnose + snapshot scan (deferred)
adv conformance status     # read-only verdict
adv worktree triage        # read-only drift report
```

No mutation subcommands in this change.

## Recommended Implementation Order

1. Extract shared CLI helper from `bin/adv` (C1): project id, external root, no-color, JSON output, disk-projection loaders, GATE_ORDER source-of-truth.
2. Add parameterized bridge-contract asset-test harness (C2).
3. Add `adv roadmap --no-color [--json]` (file mode only; annotation omitted/degraded).
4. Convert `/adv-roadmap` default path to a thin bridge once unavailable-annotation representation is decided.
5. Add spec law (`rq-roadmapCliBridge01` or general `rq-commandCliBridge01`).
6. Decide `adv validate` disk-only vs shared-validator path (C5) before implementation.
7. Defer `adv doctor`/`adv hygiene` to follow-up.

## Guardrails

- CLI disk projection is diagnostic unless a spec explicitly says it is authoritative.
- CLI never performs gate completion, task/change mutation, archive, cancellation, worktree deletion, worker restart, search-attribute registration, conformance lock/unlock/override, or snapshot repair.
- Every bridge gets spec + asset-test coverage for no-fanout/no-analysis/no-MCP-fallback AND matrix-justified-retirement.
- Temporal-dependent reads stay MCP/agent-owned until CLI has tested service access.

CONTRACT ACTIVE

- Criteria: factor matrix + dispositions for all tools/commands; matrix-justified replacement only; first safe implementation = roadmap after helper + harness.

CONTRACT FULFILLED

- Evidence: three research passes + scout, subagent reports persisted, OpenCode/Bun docs verified, factor matrix recorded.
- Status: COMPLETE
documents.problemStatement: ADV needs a factor-driven decision matrix mapping every command/tool to agent in-context need, programmability/CI value, human value, and mutation risk, then deriving the ideal surface: keep as MCP, add an additive CLI surface, move primary path to CLI, retire the MCP form when justified, or remain agent-only. Goal: reduce agent context waste and improve CI/human ergonomics without removing agent-needed tools or weakening HITL/gate boundaries.

# Agreement

## Objectives

1. Keep the factor-driven command/tool surface matrix and bridge-contract test harness delivered by the existing scope.
2. Change `adv status` from disk-projection-only to true live default: active changes must come from live Temporal-backed state before rendering.
3. Keep `/adv-status` as a thin shell-output bridge over `adv status --no-color`; the command file must not instruct agent-side ADV MCP fanout.
4. Fail closed on live-state unavailability: surface remediation such as fixing/restarting Temporal, and never silently render stale disk projections as current state.
5. Preserve existing roadmap file-snapshot bridge behavior unless later explicitly re-scoped.
6. Preserve all HITL and mutation boundaries: CLI status remains read-only and cannot complete gates, archive, cancel, mutate tasks, repair state, or restart Temporal automatically.

## Acceptance Criteria

- AC1: `adv status` default renders active changes from live Temporal-backed state, not disk projection files.
- AC2: `/adv-status` remains a thin shell-output bridge over `adv status --no-color`; command file contains no `adv_*` MCP fanout instructions.
- AC3: Stale disk-only active changes are not shown as current live active changes.
- AC4: If live Temporal status is unavailable, `adv status` exits non-zero and prints clear remediation; it does not silently fall back to a disk table.
- AC5: Standalone `adv status` outside OpenCode uses the same live-default behavior.
- AC6: `adv status --json` reports source/freshness/error fields sufficient for CI to distinguish live success from failure.
- AC7: `rq-statusCliBridge01` is updated from disk-only/no-fallback law to live-default/no-silent-stale law.
- AC8: Existing roadmap bridge/file-snapshot behavior remains unchanged unless explicitly re-scoped later.
- AC9: No CLI mutation authority is added: no gate completion, archive, cancellation, task mutation, destructive repair, or automatic Temporal restart.
- AC10: Tests cover live success, stale disk exclusion, Temporal unavailable failure, `/adv-status` bridge no-fanout contract, and spec anchor drift.

## Constraints

- Use `node:util parseArgs`; add no new heavy CLI dependencies.
- `--no-color` remains stable for slash-command bridge rendering.
- `--json` output must be CI-readable and structurally distinguish live success from live failure.
- Live status source must not silently blend stale disk-only active changes into the current active table.
- Remediation text belongs in CLI stdout/stderr/JSON, not in `.opencode/command/adv-status.md`, so bridge-command no-fanout tests remain meaningful.
- Existing roadmap CLI/file-snapshot mode is not changed by this re-entry.

## Avoidances

- Do not remove agent-needed MCP tools without a recorded matrix justification.
- Do not implement `adv validate` / `adv doctor` / `adv hygiene` in this change.
- Do not add CLI gate/archive/cancellation/task/destructive mutation authority.
- Do not emulate live Temporal status from stale disk projections.
- Do not make `/adv-status` ask the agent to call `adv_status`, `adv_change_list`, `adv_change_show`, `adv_gate_status`, or `adv_spec`.
- Do not automatically restart Temporal from `adv status`; provide remediation only.

## Preview Applicability

`visual_surface: false` — change affects CLI terminal text output, specs, and tests; no browser-visible or rendered UI surface.

## Decisions

### User Decisions

- True live default: `/adv-status` and `adv status` must pull live Temporal-backed state by default.
- Standalone CLI: `adv status` outside OpenCode also uses live default.
- Fresh over fast: prefer bounded live correctness over showing a fast stale table.
- Failure handling: when live status is unavailable, surface fix/restart Temporal guidance instead of pretending disk projections are current.

### Agent Decisions (LBP)

- Keep `/adv-status` as shell bridge; implement liveness in `bin/adv`, not in agent prompt instructions.
- Reuse existing Temporal client/address conventions where possible (`127.0.0.1:7233`, namespace default, env overrides guarded by existing policy).
- Replace the prior disk-only `rq-statusCliBridge01` law with a live-default/no-silent-stale law.
- Keep roadmap bridge scope unchanged to avoid accidental product behavior change.

## Discovery Findings

- Current `bin/adv` reads `change.json` projections from external state and can show stale/legacy active changes when projections diverge from live workflows.
- Live state is reachable through existing Temporal client conventions and existing store/list machinery, but implementation must avoid falling back to disk-only active IDs in the default status table.
- Existing spec and agreement text conflict with true-live default and have been superseded by this agreement.
- The no-fanout bridge contract remains valuable: command file stays deterministic shell-output bridge; CLI owns live status and remediation text.

## Deferred Questions

- `adv validate` disk-only vs shared-validator architecture remains deferred to a follow-up design/change.
- `adv doctor` / `adv hygiene` read-only diagnostics remain follow-up scope.

## Sign-Off

Acceptance criteria approved inline by user (`approve`) at the re-entry discovery AC checkpoint on 2026-06-05.

# Design

## Architecture Overview

`/adv-status` stays a thin OpenCode shell-output bridge:

```md
!`adv status --no-color`
```

The live-status responsibility moves into `bin/adv`. The default `adv status` path becomes a Temporal client read path, not a disk-projection reader. It resolves the ADV project ID from the git root commit, connects to Temporal using existing ADV Temporal client conventions, enumerates non-terminal change workflows through Visibility, queries each change workflow by the public query name string, and renders the existing table from queried workflow state.

Disk projections are no longer an active-row source for default status. Disk may still supply archived/closed counts, or future explicit offline inspection, but stale disk-only active changes must not appear as current live active changes.

`live` means Temporal Visibility-current plus workflow-query-current. Temporal Visibility can be eventually consistent for very fresh workflows/search-attribute updates; default status still must not replace missing live data with disk active rows.

## Key Decisions

1. **Keep command bridge thin.** `.opencode/command/adv-status.md` remains shell output + verbatim/no-analysis instructions. No `adv_*` MCP fanout appears in the command file.
2. **Live state in CLI, not agent prompt.** `bin/adv status` owns live connection, query, rendering, JSON metadata, and remediation text.
3. **Query by name string.** The CLI uses `client.workflow.getHandle(workflowId).query('adv.change.getState')` rather than importing workflow/sandbox declarations. This avoids pulling `@temporalio/workflow` sandbox-only code into the client CLI process.
4. **Visibility is the active ID source.** Enumerate active change workflow IDs using the existing Visibility query shape from `plugin/src/temporal/list-change-workflows.ts`; do not union disk active IDs into the default status table.
5. **Bound live reads.** Mirror the existing 5s query-timeout discipline from `plugin/src/storage/store-temporal/shared.ts` so a dead worker/server cannot hang `adv status` indefinitely.
6. **Fail closed, including partial query failure.** Temporal connection/list failure, timeout, or any enumerated change query failure returns whole-command non-zero with remediation; no partial/stale table and no disk active fallback.
7. **JSON is structurally explicit.** `adv status --json` includes fields such as `source: 'temporal'`, `live: true|false`, `stale: false`, `generated_at`, `error`, and `remediation` so CI can distinguish live success from failure.
8. **Roadmap unchanged.** Existing `adv roadmap` file-snapshot and `/adv-roadmap` bridge behavior remains out of this re-entry scope.
9. **No mutation authority.** Status CLI remains read-only: no gate/task/archive/cancel/destructive repair/restart operations.

## ADR Drafts

No ADR drafted. Decision is important but not hard to reverse enough to warrant a repository ADR: fallback/timeout/source behavior is pinned by specs/tests and can be changed through a future ADV change.

## Implementation Strategy

1. **Refactor status internals if needed**
   - Extract render/project/disk-count helpers from `bin/adv` into `bin/lib/*`.
   - Keep Bun-runtime tests under `bin/lib/*.test.ts`.
   - Keep plugin-side parity/asset tests as text reads, not imports from `bin/`.

2. **Add live Temporal status reader**
   - Resolve project ID with current git-root-commit logic.
   - Import/reuse safe client conventions from `plugin/src/temporal/client.ts` or duplicate a minimal equivalent if runtime packaging requires it.
   - Reuse/parallel `buildVisibilityQuery` + active statuses from `plugin/src/temporal/list-change-workflows.ts`.
   - For each `adv/change/{projectId}/{changeId}` workflow ID, query `'adv.change.getState'` by string.
   - Convert workflow state into the existing table summary shape: ID, title, task counts, gates, fast-follow parent, last activity, recency.

3. **Remove default disk active source**
   - Do not call `listChanges(root/changes)` for active rows in default `adv status`.
   - Keep disk archive reads for archived/closed counts only if needed.
   - If a future offline mode is desired, make it explicit (`--offline`) and label it projection-only; not part of current AC unless needed for tests.

4. **Failure and remediation path**
   - Connection failure: non-zero, message references Temporal server / `temporal-dev` / OpenCode worker health.
   - Visibility failure: non-zero, no disk active fallback.
   - Per-change query failure/timeout: whole-command non-zero, no partial table.
   - JSON failure payload carries error class and remediation.

5. **Spec/test updates**
   - Update `rq-statusCliBridge01` from disk-only/no-fallback law to live-default/no-silent-stale law.
   - Preserve bridge no-fanout asset test.
   - Add tests for live success, stale disk exclusion, Temporal unavailable failure, JSON metadata, partial-query failure, and query-name parity.

## LBP Analysis

This is the long-term best path because ADV state authority already lives in Temporal. A disk-only status CLI is fast but violates user expectations when projections diverge. Making the CLI a read-only Temporal client keeps the slash command cheap in agent context while aligning displayed active WIP with source-of-truth workflows.

Rejected alternatives:

- **Fix projection source only:** faster and preserves offline behavior, but still allows stale state under projection failure and does not satisfy true live default.
- **Agent-side MCP fanout in `/adv-status`:** live, but regresses the bridge goal by consuming agent context and allowing synthesis/recommendations.
- **Silent disk fallback:** convenient during Temporal outages, but violates fresh-over-fast and hides exactly the failure users need to fix.

## Affected Components

- `bin/adv` — default status implementation and JSON/failure output.
- Optional `bin/lib/*` — helpers for project resolution, rendering, live Temporal reads, disk archive counts.
- `.opencode/command/adv-status.md` — should remain materially unchanged except comments if needed; still shell bridge.
- `plugin/src/temporal/client.ts` — source of client/address/namespace conventions.
- `plugin/src/temporal/list-change-workflows.ts` — source of Visibility query semantics.
- `plugin/src/temporal/contracts.ts` — source of query-name string and workflow ID conventions.
- `.adv/specs/advance-meta/spec.json` — update `rq-statusCliBridge01`.
- `plugin/src/*cli*assets.test.ts` / new bin tests — bridge and live-status regression coverage.

## Risks / Mitigations

- **Bun dependency resolution for `@temporalio/client`:** validate with a targeted Bun test/command. If importing plugin modules is brittle, use a small bin-local live reader that imports from plugin-rooted code, or a minimal copied Temporal client convention with tests.
- **Accidental sandbox import:** forbid importing `plugin/src/temporal/messages.ts` or workflow files from `bin/` tests; query by name string only.
- **Visibility eventual consistency:** document live semantics as Visibility-current + workflow-query-current; do not use disk active fallback to mask lag.
- **Visibility search-attribute drift:** add parity/asset coverage for the query name and visibility builder or literal tokens.
- **Dead worker hangs:** wrap connection/list/query in bounded timeout; reuse 5s query-timeout rationale.
- **Partial workflow query failures:** fail closed for default status.
- **Archive counts still disk-backed:** acceptable because AC targets active current WIP; label JSON source if counts come from disk.

## Design Leverage Scout

Scout considered 5 findings and auto-adopted 3:

- Adopt query-by-name-string for `adv.change.getState` so CLI avoids workflow sandbox imports.
- Adopt active rows from Visibility + workflow queries only; do not reuse store `listSummary` because it intentionally falls back to disk.
- Adopt bounded query timeout using existing 5s query-timeout precedent.

Surfaced risks handled in design:

- Runtime resolution for `@temporalio/client` under Bun must be verified by tests.
- Add query-name/visibility parity tests so string-based client boundary does not drift.

## Validator Result

VALIDATED with two non-blocking cautions, both incorporated:

1. `live` is defined precisely as Visibility-current plus workflow-query-current; Visibility may briefly lag.
2. Partial query failure is pinned to fail-closed: whole-command non-zero with remediation, no partial/stale table.

Validator recommendation: adopt the design. It deliberately inverts the plugin `listSummary` disk fallback so CLI fails closed instead of blending stale disk rows, while preserving the thin bridge and no-mutation constraints.

# Executive Summary

## Outcome

Implemented live-default ADV CLI status behavior while preserving thin slash-command bridge boundaries. `/adv-status` still returns shell output verbatim, but `adv status` now uses Temporal-backed live state for active rows and fails closed with remediation instead of silently showing stale disk projections.

## Verdict

APPROVED

## What Was Built

1. Extracted shared `bin/lib` helpers for status/roadmap rendering, project resolution, gate order, and change summary computation.
2. Added `adv roadmap` file-snapshot CLI and `/adv-roadmap` thin bridge with no fabricated active-change annotation.
3. Added CLI surface matrix documentation and coverage tests for commands/tools.
4. Updated `rq-statusCliBridge01` to require live-default status and no silent stale active fallback.
5. Implemented `bin/lib/live-status.ts` for Temporal Visibility enumeration and `adv.change.getState` workflow queries by name string.
6. Changed `adv status` default to use live Temporal-backed active rows; disk active rows are no longer loaded for the default status table.
7. Added fail-closed status JSON/error path with `source`, `live`, `stale`, `error`, and `remediation` fields.
8. Added guards for no command-file ADV MCP fanout, no workflow sandbox imports, query-name parity, roadmap unchanged behavior, and no CLI mutation authority.
9. Updated release-facing CLI docs/comments from disk-only status to live-default status plus roadmap snapshot mode.
10. Review remediation made `bin/adv.test.ts` hermetic by adding local Git identity and deterministic quiet temp repo init.

## What Was Verified

- Verdict: APPROVED with 0 blockers and 0 unresolved issues from `adv-reviewer` acceptance review.
- Tests: `bun test bin/` passed 66 tests.
- Tests: CLI bridge/parity/matrix targeted suite passed 23 tests.
- Tests: `bin/oc-test targeted -- src/cli-bridge-contract.test.ts` passed 16 tests.
- Formatting: touched `src/cli-bridge-contract.test.ts` passed Prettier check; reviewer also reported `git diff --check` pass.
- Runtime failure path: `ADV_STATUS_TIMEOUT_MS=1000 bun bin/adv status --json` exited 2 with `live:false`, `stale:false`, `error`, and remediation; no disk table fallback was emitted.
- Full check caveat: `pnpm run check` still fails only on pre-existing unrelated Prettier warnings in three untouched files.
- Preview URL: not_applicable — CLI/spec/docs/tests only; no browser-visible visual surface.
- Contract matrix: 22 required rows persisted; all AC rows pass and all constraints/avoidances are respected.

## Remaining Concerns

Current live runtime command reports `Failed to query Workflow` and fails closed under the existing Temporal state issue, which is the intended no-stale fallback behavior. Live success may require fixing/restarting Temporal or cleaning stale workflow state before archive/release validation.
